body.title: [sprint] Unproven builders push to a branch/PR, never directly to main
body.intention: I intend that an unproven autonomous builder cannot land unreviewed work on main, so proof-on-close and the advisor-review-ladder actually gate what ships.
body.description:
**Problem** (policy, surfaced by the DS.0 observe-run analysis 2026-07-03): coding.md step 5 (L64) instructs the builder to `git push origin main`, and the AgenticLoop Bash denylist (agentic_loop.py:48) only blocks `git push --force` — so an UNPROVEN builder can push unreviewed work straight to main. That is exactly the class proof-on-close and the advisor-review-ladder (T-ds-advisor-review-ladder) exist to gate. (Not exercised in the 2026-07-03 run — it died in explore before pushing — but confirmed by reading coding.md.)
**Proposed shape:** builders push to a per-ticket branch (e.g. ds/<ticket-id>) and open a PR / hand off for review, never main; and/or the denylist refuses `git push origin main` for builder domains. Decide the mechanism (branch+PR preferred so the review/proof gate applies before landing).
**Cross-ref** (avoid overlap): the push MECHANICS — askpass guard on the push, and reconciling the double model-push-vs-listener-close ownership — already live in T-ds-builder-prompt-fixes. This ticket is POLICY-ONLY: WHERE unproven work is allowed to land (branch/PR, not main).
**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/inference/prompts/coding.md (step 5 target); possibly unseen_university/devices/inference/agentic_loop.py (denylist).
**Design rules:** proof-on-close; CP6 (build-and-care-for-safety-as-we-go).
**Scope boundary:** IN — the builder git target becomes a branch/PR and direct pushes to main are prevented for builder domains. OUT — the human/CC review-ladder itself (T-ds-advisor-review-ladder); the push askpass/double-close mechanics (T-ds-builder-prompt-fixes).
**Test plan:** the coding prompt no longer contains `git push origin main`; a guard/test asserts a builder push to main is refused (or routed to a branch).
**Completion criteria:** builders cannot land on main directly; a test or denylist rule enforces branch/PR targeting.
**Rollback:** git revert.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
